publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-22 23:46:12 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, subject files, a volume, and a photographic negative.
did.abstract: Correspondence and subject files pertaining chiefly to Fogel's career as Professor of English at Cornell.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and subject files pertaining chiefly to Fogel's career at Cornell. Letters and documents touch on departmental policies and procedures; promotion and tenure discussions; recommendations for students and colleagues; lecture notes, reading lists, syllabi. Special files concern Fogel's work in creating the Paul Gottschalk Memoral Lecture series honoring his deceased colleague. Materials relating to Fogel's own scholarly projects include substantial files on his collaboration with David V. Erdman in editing Evidence for Authorship for the Cornell University Press. Also, a volume of the complete works of Edmund Spenser owned and annotated by both Fogel and William Strunk; a photographic negative of a bookplate honoring John Alban Finch, and letter from Fogel to Elizabeth Murphy concerning the Finch Fund and bookplate.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Ephim Fogel papers, #14-12-2711. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Box 4 restricted to the permission of the University Archivist.

did.unittitle: Endel (4), original draft with Mr. Fogel's comments; appendix to chapter on anality (III)
